gaps.reason: derived nodule path `core.wrapping` has segment(s) [wrapping] colliding with a Mycelium reserved word — emitting them verbatim would fail to parse (`nodule core.wrapping;` tokenizes the colliding word as a keyword, not an identifier); each colliding segment is escaped with the `_kw` suffix (now `core.wrapping_kw`) rather than dropped, so distinct source files whose only differing segment is a reserved word (e.g. `l1.fuse` vs `l1.nodule`) stay distinguishable instead of both collapsing onto the same nodule path (G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The explicit, named Axis-B opt-out (RFC-0034 §10; M-791)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Present on a [`Meta`](crate::meta::Meta) only when the producing operation was given a"] # [doc = " **named, explicit** `wrapping` annotation at the use site. Its absence is the default state"] # [doc = " (never-silent Axis-B failability applies). Its presence marks \"the caller explicitly requested"] # [doc = " wraparound; this is visible and auditable.\""] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `WrappingOpt` does **not** alter the guarantee strength (`Exact`/`Proven`/…) or the"] # [doc = " certification mode (`Fast`/`Certified`/…); those are Axis-A and certification axes and remain"] # [doc = " orthogonal (VR-5). A `wrapping` annotation is \"I chose wraparound here, explicitly\" — it is"] # [doc = " not \"I lowered the accuracy bar\"."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct WrappingOpt { # [doc = " Reason field — reserved for v1 (M-791): a developer-supplied justification string (like the"] # [doc = " `// SAFETY:` convention for `unsafe`). In v0 the only legal value is `()` (the unit token is"] # [doc = " the explicit acknowledgement: no magic string, no default comment). The field is private so"] # [doc = " the stable public surface is just `WrappingOpt::new()`."] _reason : () , }
gaps.reason: struct `WrappingOpt` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl WrappingOpt { # [doc = " Construct the explicit `wrapping` opt-out marker (v0). The call site is the acknowledgement:"] # [doc = " the developer wrote `WrappingOpt::new()` — named, visible, grep-auditable (RFC-0034 §10)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **v1 will require a justification argument** (analogous to `// SAFETY:`). Until then, the"] # [doc = " type's existence at the use site is the disclosure."] # [must_use] pub const fn new () -> Self { WrappingOpt { _reason : () } } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (1 sub-issue(s)): impl method `new` body: struct literal `WrappingOpt { .. }` — not an in-file single-ctor struct or enum struct-variant that emits (no constructor to build; a cross-nodule variant is an honest DN-113/DN-134-OQ-2 resolvability gap, never a fabricated out-of-file reference — VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: impl Default for WrappingOpt { fn default () -> Self { Self :: new () } }
gaps.reason: no member of this impl block could be transpiled (1 sub-issue(s)): impl method `default` body: qualified/associated-function call `Self :: new` did not resolve to a known-emitted associated fn — Mycelium calls are bare identifiers, and the only sound surface form for `Type::method(...)` is the mangled `Type__method` name the declaration side already emits for a receiver-less inherent-impl associated fn (DN-34 §8.13/8.14), referenced only when that exact declaration is PROVEN present (same-file, or a resolved M-1084 cross-nodule sibling). A primitive/std associated fn with no emitted decl (e.g. `i128::try_from`), an unresolved type, or a `self`-receiving method (excluded by construction — it stays reachable only from its own bare call site) all gap here rather than fabricate a call (G2/VR-5, DN-133)
